techniques.evidence_quote: Using one of these AWS access keys, the actor was able to gain access to npm's AWS infrastructure
an attacker abused stolen OAuth user tokens issued to two third-party OAuth integrators, Heroku and Travis CI
the actor was able to exfiltrate a set of private npm repositories, some of which included secrets such as AWS access keys
private npm repositories, some of which included secrets such as AWS access keys
a subset of npm service logs stored in our internal logging system contained data that was not properly sanitized to remove credentials received in requests to npm services
the actor exfiltrated a set of npm inventory CSV files containing directory listings of the S3 buckets storing packages for the npm registry

the actor was able to exfiltrate an older backup of the skimdb.npmjs.com mirror...the actor exfiltrated a set of npm inventory CSV files containing directory listings of the S3 buckets...the actor exfiltrated a small subset of private package contents
exfiltrate an older backup of the skimdb.npmjs.com mirror, which included metadata and package manifests for all public and private packages

attack_chain.description: Attacker obtained stolen OAuth user tokens originally issued to third-party integrators Heroku and Travis CI, granting access to GitHub.com organizations including the npm organization.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: an attacker abused stolen OAuth user tokens issued to two third-party OAuth integrators, Heroku and Travis CI, to download data from dozens of GitHub.com organizations

attack_chain.description: Using stolen OAuth tokens, attacker cloned and exfiltrated a set of private npm GitHub repositories.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Using their initial foothold of OAuth user tokens for GitHub.com, the actor was able to exfiltrate a set of private npm repositories

attack_chain.description: Within the exfiltrated private repositories, the attacker found plaintext AWS access keys embedded in repository files.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a set of private npm repositories, some of which included secrets such as AWS access keys

attack_chain.description: Attacker used the harvested AWS access keys to authenticate to npm's AWS cloud infrastructure, escalating from GitHub access to AWS infrastructure access.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Using one of these AWS access keys, the actor was able to gain access to npm's AWS infrastructure

attack_chain.description: With AWS access, the attacker enumerated S3 bucket contents, obtaining directory listings of npm registry package storage.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the actor exfiltrated a set of npm inventory CSV files containing directory listings of the S3 buckets storing packages for the npm registry

attack_chain.description: Attacker exfiltrated multiple datasets from npm's AWS cloud storage: a skimdb.npmjs.com backup (containing ~100k user records from 2015, package manifests and metadata), S3 inventory CSVs listing all private package names/versions, and private package contents from two organizations.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the actor was able to exfiltrate an older backup of the skimdb.npmjs.com mirror...the actor exfiltrated a set of npm inventory CSV files...the actor exfiltrated a small subset of private package contents belonging to two specific organizations

initial_access_vector: Stolen third-party OAuth user tokens (Heroku and Travis CI integrations) used to access GitHub.com organizations, including the npm organization's private repositories
privilege_escalation: AWS access keys discovered in exfiltrated private GitHub repositories were used to authenticate directly to npm's AWS infrastructure (T1078.004)
